_meta.name: Exhibit Opportunity Map
_meta.purpose: Priority map for new exhibit families ranked by agent design difficulty, user articulation difficulty, and strategic leverage for EXHIBIT.

selectionLens.label: Agent Construction Difficulty
selectionLens.definition: How hard the pattern is for an agent to design correctly without strong precedent.

selectionLens.label: User Articulation Difficulty
selectionLens.definition: How hard the need is for users to describe clearly without already knowing UI system language.

selectionLens.label: Product Leverage
selectionLens.definition: How much the exhibit family improves /api/agent, the coherence auditor, and EXHIBIT as a system.

families.title: Async Tab Transition Patterns
whyItMatters: Users describe blanking, jank, or weird refresh behavior without naming transition structure.
Agents routinely fail to specify stale-vs-skeleton-vs-blocking behavior for tab switches.
candidateExhibits: segmented tabs with panel skeleton replacement
async tab rail with focus preservation

families.title: Dropdown vs Segmented Control Decision Patterns
whyItMatters: This is one of the clearest recurring coherence failures in AI-generated UI.
Users feel it immediately but usually describe it only as clunky or too many clicks.
candidateExhibits: anti-pattern comparison for dropdown over visible mode switching
segmented control for high-frequency state switching
compact decision matrix for when dropdowns are still justified

families.title: Loading Transition Timing Systems
whyItMatters: Agents rarely know when to use instant swap, skeleton, stale-preserving refresh, or blocking spinner.
This is a major hidden quality gap in otherwise polished output.
candidateExhibits: timing ladder exhibit: instant, light delay, sustained load, failed load
skeleton threshold examples for table, inspector, and thread surfaces

families.title: Optimistic UI and Rollback Patterns
whyItMatters: Users describe trust failure, not rollback structure.
Agents often skip temporary state communication and failure reversal.
candidateExhibits: optimistic save button with pending and failure states
mutation timeline showing pending, committed, and failed branches

families.title: Focus and Scroll Preservation Systems
whyItMatters: Users say the UI jumps around or loses their place.
Agents rarely specify focus return and viewport stability.
candidateExhibits: right rail refresh with preserved scroll
async panel replacement without context loss

families.title: Duplicate Control Anti-Pattern Gallery
whyItMatters: This is central to the UI coherence auditor.
Agents need explicit negative references, not only polished references.
candidateExhibits: repeated filter meaning across chips and dropdowns
redundant status language across header, chip, and footer
conflicting global vs local sort or mode controls

families.title: Async State Machine Exhibits
candidateExhibits: thread loading and send state machine
form submit and retry state machine

families.title: Inline Editing Systems
candidateExhibits: inline title edit with save and rollback
inline field validation under live constraints

families.title: Async Confirmation Flows
candidateExhibits: bulk approve confirmation with async outcome

families.title: Error Recovery Systems Beyond Toasts
candidateExhibits: partial table failure with row-level retry
failed background sync with manual retry
fatal panel error with preserved context and escape path

families.title: Multi-Step Form Systems With Async Friction
candidateExhibits: step-by-step setup flow with async validation

families.title: Permission-Shaped Surfaces
candidateExhibits: hidden vs disabled vs explanatory permission treatment

families.title: Cross-Surface Hand-Off Patterns
candidateExhibits: queue to inspector to confirm flow
assistant prompt to routed workspace flow
alert to exact failing object flow
export setup to result artifact flow

families.title: Header Composition Patterns
candidateExhibits: title, status, scope, and cta ownership rules

families.title: Persistent Text Value Tests
candidateExhibits: useless footer copy vs useful state copy
redundant helper text vs clarifying helper text
internal system language vs productized language

families.title: Density Discipline Comparisons
candidateExhibits: one task in compact, default, and broken mixed-density form
bloated card-heavy version of the same screen

families.title: Surface Bloat Reduction Patterns
candidateExhibits: too many panels vs one dominant work surface
excessive summary cards vs compressed status line
noisy label stacks vs semantic economy pass
